fields.description: Authentication mechanism for this SASL user. SCRAM-SHA-512 is recommended for production; SCRAM-SHA-256 is compatible with older clients.
Name of the Kubernetes Secret that contains this user's password. The operator reads this Secret and creates the SASL user in Redpanda with the specified credentials.
Key within the referenced Secret that holds the password value.
List of Kafka ACL rules for this user. Each rule specifies a resource type (Topic, Group, TransactionalID), resource name pattern, operation (Read, Write, All, etc.), and permission (Allow, Deny).

Controls whether the SASL user is deleted from Redpanda when the User CR is deleted. 'delete' removes the user from the broker; 'retain' leaves the user account intact. Use 'retain' to safely remove the CR while keeping the user for existing client connections.
Kafka broker addresses (host:port) the operator connects to when creating or updating this SASL user.
Enable TLS for the operator's Kafka connection when managing this user.
Enable SASL for the operator's Kafka connection (the operator itself authenticates to manage users).
SASL mechanism for the operator's own Kafka connection.
Secret containing 'username' and 'password' for the operator's admin Kafka connection used to manage this user.
